reason: The statement applies because the text describes a series of tragic events inflicted upon the Egyptians as part of the plagues sent by the LORD through Moses and Aaron to convince Pharaoh to let the Israelites go. These events include turning the waters into blood, infesting the land with frogs, lice, flies, killing the cattle, causing boils, hail, darkness, and ultimately resulting in the death of the firstborn in Egypt, leading to a great cry and mourning throughout the land.
